'''Matt Dunford''' (born 4 March 1968) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s.  Dunford played for [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]] in the [[New South Wales Rugby League|NSWRL]], and the [[Australian Rugby League|ARL]] and [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
Dunford made his first grade debut for Manly-Warringah in round 1 [[1990 NSWRL season|1990]] against [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]] at [[Leichhardt Oval]]. In the same year, Dunford played in Manly's Minor Semi-Final loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos]].

In [[1991 NSWRL season|1991]], Dunford played 21 games for Manly, mostly as their starting hooker. Unfortunately this also included both of their finals matches against [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]] and [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] which ended in defeat. For Manly it was a sad exit for the [[Graham Lowe]] coached side who had finished the minor round in 2nd place, but the late season loss of [[Des Hasler]] and [[Ian Roberts (rugby league)|Ian Roberts]] to injury couldn't be overcome. Dunford subsequently played for Manly in their [[1994 NSWRL season|1994]] finals campaign where they were once again defeated by Brisbane.

In the [[1995 ARL season]], Dunford played 9 games for Manly as the club won the minor premiership losing only twice in the process but did not feature in their finals campaign or grand final loss to Canterbury. In the [[1996 ARL season]], Dunford only featured in 2 games for Manly as they won the minor premiership for a second consecutive year.<ref>Alan Whiticker/Glen Hudson: The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players. (1995 edition) {{ISBN|1875169571}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/northern-beaches/blindside-it-was-league8217s-big-name-family-affair-at-brookie/news-story/b8c526d4fb0a4294e810de3eb52753b6/|title=It was league's big name family affair at Brookie|website=Daily Telegraph}}</ref>

In 1997, Dunford signed for English side the [[London Broncos]] and played two seasons with them before retiring. His son Zane (b.2002) currently plays for Manly's [[NSW Cup]] feeder club, the [[Blacktown Workers |Blacktown Workers Sea Eagles]].<ref>[https://www.seaeagles.com.au/teams/nsw-cup/manly-warringah-sea-eagles/zane-dunford/ Zane Dunford profile]</ref>
